observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: a) Direct the General Services Officer with the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count and classify properly the accounts presented in the RPCPPE; b) Direct the concerned department to complete the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts intended to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability; and c) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to reconcile the balances per financial statements against the RPCPPE prepared by the General Services Office and make full disclosures of the related information in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: The management had already started the process of one-time cleansing of PPEs. No reports had been submitted by the GSO and no adjustments had been made yet by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the following: a) Municipal General Services Officer: i. Maintain proper and complete records of the PPEs, particularly the stock cards and property cards in compliance with the applicable rules and regulations; ii. Complete the disposal procedures of the remaining unserviceable properties in coordination with the accounting department to derecognize the same in the books of the agency. a. Maintain a manual PPE Ledger Cards to record the custody, estimated useful life, depreciation, impairment and disposal of those assets;
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: The GSO had not maintained the records such as stock cards and property cards. The management will conduct the disposal of the remaining unserviceable properties once the one-time cleansing process has been done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: a) Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and other offices involved in implementing the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure the implementation of all the programs, projects and activities included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan; b) Direct the concerned personnel (Special Disbursing Officer) to liquidate the cash advance charged under the 30% Quick Response Fund and direct the Accounting Office to submit the liquidation report together with the supporting documents to the Audit Team; and c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the amounts disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements provide accurate details of the utilized and unutilized balances of the LDRRM Fund in accordance with 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: The management: a) Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and other offices involved in implementing the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure the implementation of all the programs, projects and activities included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the amounts disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements provide accurate details of the utilized and unutilized balances of the LDRRM Fund and regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance with Items 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management, together with the MPDC and Municipal Engineering to: a) Address the causes of delay and/or non-implementation of various projects funded under 20% EDF and ensure that all programs and projects are implemented effectively; b) Revisit the AIP for the year as well as the continuing appropriations for projects of prior years and reevaluate the relevance of said projects to the present priority needs of the municipality; c) Set a specific timeline for the implementation of each program, project or activity of the 20% EDF; and d) Evaluate the technical viability of proposed development projects and monitor the proper and timely implementation of 20% DF programs to attain optimal socio-economic and environmental management outcomes.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: There are projects that are still unimplemented as of year-end. Untimely preparation and approval of projects and programs causing the partial implementation of PPAs funded under 20% EDF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned department heads to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements set forth under the COA Circular 2015-008 as follows: 1. Municipal General Services Office – to maintain Local Road Inventory as well as Road Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, as well as prepare a Report on Local Road Network as part of its duties and responsibilities; 2. Inventory Committee – to promptly conduct an inventory of the road network system, and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System; and 3. Municipal Engineering Office – to provide the Accounting Office and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of the required documents from the offices concerned.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: The GSO had not maintained the Local Road Inventory and Road Map. No inventory count has been made by the management as regard to its Road Network accounts. The inventory of this account will be included on the one time cleansing process. Municipal Engineering has not maintained records on the local roads of the LGU. The basis of the listing of the local roads were the subsidiary ledgers maintained by the accounting office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to allocate sufficient funds for the establishment of the sanitary landfill and expedite its construction in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: Still working for titling / preparation of plans & design. Construction of the sanitary landfill (SLF) not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: Still working for titling.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management:
a) Direct the Inventory Committee created by the LGU to expedite the conduct of
physical count of all PPE and reconciliation of PPE records, in accordance with
the guidelines and procedures in the one-time cleansing of PPE account
provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish the
validity, existence and correctness of the PPE balances;
b) Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to eliminate
unreconciled balances on the Accounting records and reconcile the accounting
and GSO records;
c) Require the Municipal Engineering Office to provide a copy of Certificate of
Completion upon completion of the projects to Office of the Municipal
Accountant;
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to:
i. Establish the existence and whereabouts of the land that cannot be
determined;
ii. Discontinue the practice of transferring the completed projects at year-
end and instead record the transfer of these projects at the time of their
completion;
iii. Revert the entries made on the transfer of on-going projects to
Construction in Progress – Infrastructure Assets; and
iv. Prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the depreciation of the
completed project for CY 2024.

We recommended that the management:
a. Fully implement the increase in capitalization threshold from P15,000.00 to
P50,000.00 and direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services
Officer (GSO) to undertake necessary procedures provided under COA Circular
2022-004;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to:
i. Reclassify the remaining properties from PPE accounts to Semi-
Expendable Property accounts; and
ii. Recognize the corresponding expense account for the properties that
have already been issued to the end-users.

We recommended that the management:
a) Deposit all the collections promptly with the Authorized Government Depository
Bank (AGDB) in compliance with Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs and
Section 69(1) of P.D. 1445;
b) Establish a clear cut-off policy for year-end closing, particularly regarding
collections that need to be deposited at year-end;
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
records and take-up necessary adjusting entries in their respective records; and
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the reconciling items in the books to
reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) direct the concerned accountable
officers to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances, issue or send demand
letters as necessary; and (b) If no settlement received within the prescribed period
cause the withholding of salary and adhere strictly with the rules and regulations on
the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed under Section
89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Management, together with the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineering to:
a) Establish a specific/clear timeline for the implementation of each program,
projects and activities under 20% Development Fund;
b) Address the causes of delay and/or non-implementation of various projects
funded under 20% Development Fund and to include only projects that can
realistically be started and completed within the year; and
c) Submit the status of the continuing appropriations to the Audit Team for
verification.

We recommended that the Management:
1) Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO)
and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and other offices
involved in implementing the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund to ensure the implementation of all the programs, projects and activities
included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan;
2) Direct the concerned personnel (Special Disbursing Officer) to liquidate the
cash advance charged under the 30% Quick Response Fund and direct the
Accounting Office to submit the liquidation report together with the supporting
documents to the Audit Team; and
3) Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Officer (LDRRMO) to ensure that the amounts disclosed in the
Notes to Financial Statements provides accurate details of the utilized and
unutilized balances of the LDRRM Fund in accordance with 5.1.16 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the management agreed to: a) Send written notice to the payees informing them about the stale checks cancellation or replacement in accordance with applicable laws, rules and regulations; and b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to revert back to cash accounts those stale checks as of end of the year.
observations.managementAction: AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management:
a) Direct the General Services Officer with the Inventory Committee to conduct
complete physical count and classify properly the accounts presented in the
RPCPPE;
b) Direct the concerned department to complete the one-time cleansing of PPE
accounts intended to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition, and accountability; and
c) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries
in order to reconcile the balances per financial statements against the RPCPPE
prepared by the General Services Office and make full disclosures of the related
information in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a) Send written notice to the payees informing them about the stale check for
cancellation or replacement in accordance with applicable laws, rules and
regulations; and
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to
revert back to cash accounts those stale checks as of end of the year.

We recommended that the management:
a) Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO)
and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to set a
specific timeline for the implementation of each program, project or activity of the
LDRMMF; and
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the amounts disclosed in the
Notes to Financial Statements provide accurate details of the utilized and
unutilized balances of the LDRRM Fund and regularly submit the monthly
Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices,
in accordance with Items 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the management, together with the MPDC and Municipal
Engineering to:
a) Address the causes of delay and/or non-implementation of various projects
funded under 20% EDF and ensure that all programs and projects are
implemented effectively.
b) Revisit the AIP for the year as well as the continuing appropriations for
projects of prior years and reevaluate the relevance of said projects to the
present priority needs of the municipality.
c) Set a specific timeline for the implementation of each program, project or
activity of the 20% EDF.

observations.managementAction: and the Management agreed to direct the following:
observations.reason: See Part II of this Report

observations.managementAction: and the Management agreed to:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended AAR and management agreed to:
a) Address the causes of delay and/or non-implementation of various projects funded under 20% and properly plan and supervise the implementation of infrastructure projects in accordance with the approved plan and timelines to attain the desired economic growth as objective of the project;
b) Revisit the AIP for the year as well as the continuing appropriations for projects not implemented for prior years and reevaluate the relevance of said projects to the present priority needs of the LGU; and
c) Evaluate the technical viability of proposed development projects and monitor the proper and timely implementation of 20% DF programs to attain optimal socio-economic and environmental management outcomes.
observations.managementAction: and management agreed:
Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the ensuing department heads the enumerated courses of actions:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned department heads to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements set forth under the COA Circular 2015-008 as follows:
1. Municipal General Services Office – to maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, as well as prepare a Report on Local Road Network as part of its duties and responsibilities; 
2. Inventory Committee – to promptly conduct an inventory of the road network system, and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System; and
3. Municipal Engineering Office – to provide the Accounting Office and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of the required documents from the offices concerned.
observations.managementAction: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned department heads to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements set forth under the COA Circular 2015-008 as follows:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads:
1) Address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development programs and projects under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development of the LGU and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.
2) Revisit the AIP for the year as well as the continuing appropriations for projects of prior years and reevaluate the relevance of said projects to the present priority needs of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: We recommended that Management, with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads:

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the General Services Office, should reconcile the records of the LGU properties and make appropriate adjustments in books to ascertain the accuracy in the presentation of the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the General Services Office, should reconcile the records of the LGU properties and make appropriate adjustments in books to ascertain the accuracy in the presentation of the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed, with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads, to address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development programs and projects under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development of the LGU and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.
observations.managementAction: and Management agreed, with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads, to address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development programs and projects under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development of the LGU and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to allocate sufficient funds for the establishment of the sanitary landfill and expedite its construction in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: and Management agreed to allocate sufficient funds for the establishment of the sanitary landfill and expedite its construction in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended AAR and Management agreed on the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant should issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation.

Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation.

observations.managementAction: and Management agreed, with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads, to address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development programs and projects under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development of the LGU and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to allocate sufficient funds for the establishment of the sanitary landfill and expedite its construction in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: and Management agreed to allocate sufficient funds for the establishment of the sanitary landfill and expedite its construction in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all Land Improvements and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO should gather the relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items and take up the necessary adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus.
observations.managementAction: We recommended to the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all Land Improvements and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive to direct the ensuing department heads the enumerated course of actions:
To the Municipal Accountant:
a. Compute or trace any discrepancy with the RPCPPE and prepare proper
adjusting entries to correct the same;
b. Maintain a manual PPE Ledger Cards to record the custody, estimated useful
life, depreciation, impairment and disposal of those assets;
c. Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner,
through proper time management and sound internal control, in order to
achieve a fair presentation of financial statements.
To the Municipal General Service Officer:
a. Conduct a complete inventory count of all the properties of the Local
Government.
b. Maintain proper and complete records of the PPEs, particularly the stock
cards and property cards in compliance with the applicable rules and
regulations;
c. Conduct proper procedures to dispose of the unserviceable properties, in
coordination with the accounting department to derecognize the same in the
books of the agency; and,
d. Institute measures in the monitoring and controlling of the properties of the
LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned
department heads to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements set forth
under the COA Circular 2015-008 as follows:
1. Municipal Accounting Office ������������� �� �� - to prepare and maintain subsidiary
ledger cards for Local Road Network, provide depreciation separately
to each component of the road network with no provision for residual
value and disclose the road network system in the Notes to Financial
Statements in accordance with the prescribed format. Moreover,
prepare necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the error made in
the computation of accumulated depreciation.
2. Municipal General Services Office ������ � - to maintain Local Road Inventory and Road
Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, as well as
prepare a Report on Local Road Network as part of its duties and
responsibilities;
3. Inventory Committee ����������� - to promptly conduct an inventory of the road
network system, and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System; and
4. Municipal Engineering Office ������ - to provide the Accounting Office and
GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of the
required documents from the offices concerned.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reconstruct the Cash Flows Statements manually so that accurate and reliable
information are presented.
b. Check, verify and review transactions affecting the disposals and acquisitions of
PPEs to ensure proper presentation of accounts on the face of the financial
statements.
c. Evaluate and revisit the electronic accounting system employed by the LGU to
avoid similar errors in the future.

We recommended that Management, with the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads:
1) Address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all
priority development programs and projects under the 20% LDF that are
envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental
development of the LGU and directly supportive of the Philippine
Development Plan and Public Investment Program.
2) Revisit the AIP for the year as well as the continuing appropriations for projects of prior
years and reevaluate the relevance of said projects to the present priority needs of the
municipality.

We recommended and that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare the accounting journal entries to recognize the unrecorded assets to ensure that the properties are duly accounted in the books and effect the prior period adjusting entry for the unrecorded expenses to present fairly the amount.
b. Further improve the recording of succeeding transactions affecting the bayanihan grant using the appropriate accounts as prescribed in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office has recorded the assets in the books as end of CY 2020 and set up Due to National agency for the remaining accounts in the financial statements;

observations.recommendation: We recommended and require the Local Finance Committee (LFC), in coordination with the other local officials concerned, to prepare a monthly utilization report regardless of the amount expended and regularly post it in at least three conspicuous public spaces and to LGU website to keep the community informed and updated on the use of public funds.
observations.managementAction: The utilization reports were submitted monthly and were posted to LGU website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and that the LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, must prepare a complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the current appropriations and the unexpended previous years’ balances. The Municipal Accountant should record all procurement and issuance of supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that cash advances to be used for DRRM-related expenses should only be granted to permanently appointed employees as disbursing officer.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM officer prepared complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which include both the current appropriations and the unexpended prior years’ balances. The accounting office recorded the appropriate account which is charged to LDRRM Fund and maintained ledger cards to monitor the movement of the balances. The cash advances used for DRRM related expenses were granted to permanent employees as disbursing officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the following:
a) The Municipal Treasurer require the collectors to accomplish RCDs and turnover collections at the close of each business day and to deposit intact all collections with authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day; and
b) The Municipal Treasurer prepare a refund to source agency and close the bank account deposit and close the bank account.
c) The Municipal Accountant make adjustment on the stale checks at yearend to reflect the correct balance of cash in bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: Ensured prompt liquidation of collections and deposited it to authorized depository bank daily or the next banking day. Closed the account General fund account. Waiting for the account no. of Japan Embassy where the deposit should be deposited. The accounting office made adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and that the Municipal Accountant, with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer as lead, submit list of recipients indicating therein the specific relief goods distributed and received with dates of distribution/receipt, among other information to make possible and proper accounting for the relief operations procured and conducted. It may also help validate whether the activities benefited the targeted beneficiaries as planned.
observations.managementAction: We will submit the list of the recipients.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account for the 20% Local Development Fund as mandated by law and required by sound financial management.
observations.managementAction: The management opened an account intended for 20% EDF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer conduct a reassessment on the families/households on their eligibility in receiving the social amelioration assistance. In case the families/households are ineligible, the amount received shall be refunded by the accountable LGU officials. The Municipal Accountant submit the duly filed out social amelioration card and actual photos of the beneficiaries upon receiving the cash assistance as supporting documents on the liquidation of the funds transferred by the DSWD.
observations.managementAction: We will submit the incomplete attachment and refund for the 7 ineligible beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the General Services Office, reconcile the records of the LGU properties and make appropriate adjustments in books to ascertain the accuracy in the presentation of the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The accounting and GSO were trying to reconcile their updated records.
observations.reason: Complete physical inventory and reconciliation of PPE accounts were not facilitated

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant should issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation.
b. The Municipal Treasurer must ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before preparing and releasing the check to the accountable officer concerned.
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer should ensure that only permanently appointed employees are designated as disbursing officers.
observations.managementAction: Verbal and written reminders of previous years cash advances liquidation were given to unliquidated cash employees.
observations.reason: Lacking supporting document to support cash advances liquidation of prior years' cash advances

observations.recommendation: We recommended and that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads, to address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development programs and projects under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development of the LGU and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.
observations.managementAction: To strengthen the project monitoring committee in the conduct of project monitoring activity.
observations.reason: Full implementation was affected by pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and that the LGU allocate sufficient funds for the establishment of the sanitary landfill and expedite its construction in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: Procured equipment for residual that has the potential to be repurpose, reorganize the EEWMB to function through issuance of Executive Order.
observations.reason: Construction of SLF was not yet accomplished

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all Land Improvements and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO should gather the relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items and take up the necessary adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus.
observations.managementAction: Conduct physical inventory of land improvements and take up necessary adjustments as soon as inventory committee will be created
observations.reason: Complete physical inventory and reconciliation of PPE accounts were not facilitated

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The personnel in charge facilitate the process for titling
observations.reason: Still working for titling process

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the General Services Office, should reconcile the records of the LGU properties and make appropriate adjustments in books to ascertain the accuracy in the presentation of the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and GSO were trying to reconcile their records.
observations.reason: records of PPE were not regularly updated.

observations.recommendation:
a. The Municipal Accountant should issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation.
b. The Municipal Treasurer must ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before preparing and releasing the check to the accountable officer concerned.
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer should ensure that only permanently appointed employees are designated as disbursing officers.
observations.managementAction: Verbal and written reminders of previous years’ cash advances were given to employees.
observations.reason: Incomplete supporting documents for liquidation.

observations.recommendation:
a. The Municipal Treasurer must ensure prompt liquidation of collections by the collectors and to deposit intact all collections with authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.
b. The Local Chief Executive should cause the establishment of written guidelines and procedures in the management of petty cash fund to ensure compliance when such fund will be reestablished.
observations.managementAction: Collectors were only required to liquidate at least once a week.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Finding No. 2.a of Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
a. The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, must prepare a complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the current appropriations and the unexpended previous years’ balances.
b. The Municipal Accountant, moving forward and in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, must facilitate the immediate reconciliation of accounts and transfer all unexpended/unobligated amount for the transfer of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund account.
c. The Municipal Accountant should take up the necessary adjusting entry to record the receipts of cash assistance from other LGUs and other sources to Trust Liabilities- Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
d. The Municipal Accountant should record all procurement and issuance of supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances.
e. The Inventory Committee, through the Municipal General Services Officer, must conduct the physical count of inventories. Likewise, the Municipal General Services Officer shall maintain stock cards and submit the reconciled inventory report to the resident auditor.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer noted that they must prepare complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which include both the current appropriations and the unexpended prior years balance. The Municipal Accountant must facilitate the immediate reconciliation of the amount for the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated amount to the special trust account at the end of the current year. Also, the accounting should take up the necessary adjusting entries to record the receipts of cash assistance from other LGUs.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Finding No. 3.a and 3.b of Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed, with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads, to address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development programs and projects under the 20% LDF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Office as the implementing offices in the infrastructure project have taken their best to implement efficiently and possible within the target timelines the programs and projects funded under the 20% LDF, but some factors being considered of the experienced of some delays of the implementation like unexpended bad weather condition, natural calamities and the contribution of peace and order problem that cause delays especially those projects located in the highly influence areas of the community group operating in the municipality.
observations.reason: Full implementation was affected by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to allocate sufficient funds for the establishment of the sanitary landfill and expedite its construction in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: The management will allocate funds for the establishment of the sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Construction of the sanitary landfill was not yet accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all Land Improvements and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO should gather the relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items and take up the necessary adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus.
observations.managementAction: Conduct physical inventory of land improvements and take up necessary adjusting entries as soon as inventory committee will be created.
observations.reason: Inventory and reconciliation of land improvements and PPE accounts were not facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The personnel in-charge facilitate the process for titling.
observations.reason: Still working for titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to create a Project Monitoring Team (PMT) to undertake regular monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely and proper implementation of all the prioritized programs and projects under the 20% EDF. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to allow prompt corrective action.
observations.managementAction: Created PMT to undertake regular monitoring of project implementation and implement strategic measures to ensure timely and proper implementation.
observations.reason: Full implementation was affected by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.managementAction: The LGU appropriated funds CY 2016 for the processing /transfer of Title.
observations.reason: LGU is still on the process for the Transfer of Title.

observations.recommendation:
1) To ensure strict utilization of LDRRM funds for calamity/disaster-related programs/activities in order to align with the funds’ purpose and objectives.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management demand full liquidation of outstanding cash advances from concerned officers and employees and apply stern measures such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand or stop the granting of additional cash advance. observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
In view of the foregoing, we reiterated prior year’s recommendation for the inventory
committee to conduct complete physical count of all PPE and immediately reconcile the
report with the accounting records to ensure fair presentation of the balances of PPE
accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation
In view of the foregoing, we recommended to the Inventory Committee to conduct physical
inventory of all Land Improvements and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting
records.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO should gather the
relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items and take up the necessary
adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus.

Recommendation
We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of
the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party
claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.

Recommendations:
a. To ensure strict utilization of LDRRM funds for calamity/disaster-related
programs/activities in order to align with the funds’ purpose and objectives.
b. To revisit, review, and evaluate the programs/projects/activities prepared in the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP) of the Municipality to ensure effective formulation implementation
and utilization of LDRRM Fund.

Recommendation
We recommended to the management to create a Project Monitoring Team (PMT) to
undertake regular monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely and proper
implementation of all the prioritized programs and projects under the 20% EDF so that the desired
socio-economic development and environmental outcomes could redound to its intended
beneficiaries at the soonest time possible.

Also, the Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer
should closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring
procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to
assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to
allow prompt corrective action.

In view of the foregoing, we
recommended to the
Inventory Committee to
conduct physical inventory
of all Land Improvements
and Infrastructure Assets
and reconcile the results of
the count with the
accounting records.
Moreover, the Municipal
Accountant in coordination
with the GSO should gather
the relevant documents and
complete details of the
subject PPE items and take
up the necessary
adjustments on depreciation
and accumulated surplus.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017
observations.reason: Reiterated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the inventory committee to conduct complete physical count
of all PPE and immediately reconcile the report with the accounting records to ensure fair
presentation of the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended to prepare the RPCPPE by type to easily facilitate the
reconciliation with the accounting records. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to the Inventory Committee to conduct physical
inventory of all Land Improvements and Infrastructure Assets and reconcile the results of the count
with the accounting records.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO should gather the
relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items and take up the necessary
adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare promptly all necessary adjusting
and correcting entries to record all valid reconciling items reflected in the BRS. Moreover,
documentation from the bank should be persistently secured to serve as basis for adjustments and
corrections.

We recommend that the Management, through the Municipal Planning and Development
Council Officer and Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of the proposed
programs and projects. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress
or delays in the implementation to assist the management in the performance review and in
identifying gaps in implementation to allow prompt corrective action.

We recommended that the Management demand full liquidation of outstanding cash
advances from concerned officers and employees and apply stern measures such as withholding
the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or
decline to comply with the demand or stop the granting of additional cash advance.
Ensure also strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid
accumulation of unsettled cash advances.

We recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:
a. Observe strictly the requirement set forth in Section 7 (b) of the Republic Act No. 10410
by allocating in Local School Board Budget adequate funds for various activities in
support of the implementation of the ECCD Program, and comply with the guidelines
on allowable charges against the Special Education Fund set forth in Section 4 of Joint
Circular No. 1. s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG; and
b. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the local ECCD Council, Child
Development Teachers, Day Care Workers and other stakeholders of the ECCD System
in planning and budget preparation for the implementation of the ECCD program.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following: 

a.	The Inventory Team should prioritize the reconciliation of the result of the inventory count with the property and accounting records.  Likewise, 
the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively.

b.	The office/ department head concerned and the MGSO should facilitate the preparation of the inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for those items identified and declared as unserviceable or obsolete properties. Comply strictly with Section 79 of P.D. 1445 and other pertinent rules and regulation on the disposal of unserviceable and obsolete properties.
 
c.	For tangible assets with estimated useful life of more than one year but small enough to be considered as property, plant and equipment, the OIC Municipal Accountant should effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The GSO has assigned personnel as inventory committee.  They have conducted regular inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment every last week of June and December of the year and submitted reports to COA.
observations.reason: Only partial physical count of municipal properties was conducted during the year. The inventory items were not presented in accordance with their proper account classifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management should comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, entitled: “ Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions” and DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5-A dated October 7, 2004 in the granting of honoraria.
observations.managementAction: The management has submitted the necessary documents to support the claim of honoraria.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: The BAC has prepared and submitted the Procurement Monitoring Report to the Government Procurement and Policy Board.
observations.reason: The PMR was not yet upload in the GPPB website.
